what_it_does: This JSON file is the declared, reconfigurable configuration for the guided-review 'generate' step. It supplies the model, mode, instruction template, feedback filter, routing rules, and default route that the runtime/generate_mockup.py engine reads at call time.
observations: It is described as the single source of truth for generate_mockup.py behavior.
All prompt/model/mode values live in config rather than engine code.
It is read fresh at call time, so edits take effect without restart.
Default mode is 'plan' (safe/read-only); 'apply' is the change-making mode.
The instruction template is generate-specific and is wrapped elsewhere with a universal standards block and authorized-scope line.
Feedback filter defaults to 'pending', so only un-applied notes drive edits.
Routing 'mockup_edit' is enabled and scoped to a single mockup file via a placeholder.
Routing 'live' is declared but not implemented and would raise NotImplementedError if used.
It is positioned as a sibling of design/_system/tokens.json and addresses.json in a design-system registry.
standouts: Safe-by-default posture: mode='plan' prevents accidental changes.
Scope is intentionally restricted to one mockup file.
The file separates the generate-specific core instruction from repo-wide standards and scope enforcement.
A future 'live' route is declared as config even though the engine does not implement it yet.
conventions: Uses JSON with paired '_doc' fields for inline documentation.
Keeps behavioral literals out of code and in data.
Reads configuration fresh at call time rather than caching at startup.
Separates core template from wrapper layers (standards block, scope line).
concerns: The model value 'default' is opaque; its resolution depends on external dispatcher configuration.
The 'live' route is declared but enabled=false and not implemented, which could confuse readers about readiness.
The instruction template says 'apply every PENDING note' while the feedback_filter is 'pending'; a mismatch would arise if the filter were changed without updating the template text.
Scope uses a placeholder that is not validated inside this file.
There is no explicit version or migration field in the config.
notes: This is the data-driven control surface for the generate step: it decides which model/mode to use, how to render the prompt, which feedback is actionable, and which file the engine may touch.
questions: How does the dispatcher resolve the 'default' model identifier?
What is the exact format and source of the {feedback_block} content?
How will the 'live' route differ from 'mockup_edit' once implemented?
Where and how is the scope diff enforced against the declared scope?
Does switching to 'apply' mode require an explicit git revert workflow, and where is that documented?
How do sibling files tokens.json and addresses.json relate to this config at runtime?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is the declared, reconfigurable heart of the generate step and the single source of truth for generate_mockup.py behavior.
apparent_themes: Separation of core instruction from universal standards
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the generate step fully data-driven, safely editable without code changes, and tightly scoped to a single mockup file, while leaving explicit seams for future model pinning and a live-build route.
novelty: The instruction template is deliberately only the generate-specific core, with the understanding that another layer wraps it with repo-wide standards and scope enforcement; also, a not-yet-implemented 'live' route is carried as declared config rather than being omitted.
contribution: It contributes a declarative, fresh-read control plane for the generate step, including a safe default mode, a single-file scope rule, a placeholder-based instruction template, and explicit documentation fields that keep behavior transparent and editable as data.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON configuration file in company/design/_system that controls the guided-review generate step by declaring model, mode, instruction template, feedback filter, routing, and scope for runtime/generate_mockup.py.
intention_for_embedding: To make generate-step behavior fully reconfigurable through data, with safe defaults and tightly scoped single-file edits, while documenting seams for future model and live-route wiring.
